name: Honda Civic Si (8th Gen)
reliability: 8/10 — the 8th-gen Si got the strong K20Z3 (197 hp, revving to 8,000 rpm) with a slick 6-speed and, for the first time on an Si, a factory helical limited-slip differential. The K-series is durable and the chain-driven valvetrain means no timing belt. Two known-but-not-universal issues keep it off a 9: a small number of 2006–2009 blocks developed cracks around a coolant passage, and 3rd-gear synchro wear shows up on hard-shifted cars. Buy on service history and it's a superb, tunable daily.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Moderate-high (sport compact, young-driver target) · Maintenance ~$450/yr · Strong, stable values — a clean 8th-gen Si is a sought-after modern classic
popular_mods: Intake + header + cat-back exhaust — the K20Z3 responds well and sounds great
A tune (Ktuner / Hondata) to sharpen throttle and VTEC
Coilovers + sway bars to exploit the chassis and factory LSD
A short shifter and fresh bushings for crisper 6-speed throws
Summer performance tires — the LSD chassis can use more grip than stock

generations.8th Gen FG2 Coupe (2006–2011): The two-door Si — 197 hp K20Z3, 6-speed, helical LSD. The enthusiast's pick of the generation.
generations.8th Gen FA5 Sedan (2007–2011): The first-ever Si sedan, mechanically identical to the coupe — practical without giving up the drivetrain.
generations.Mugen Si (2008): A limited-run, Mugen-tuned FA5 sedan with unique suspension, aero, and wheels — the collectible of the generation.

common_issues: Rare K20Z3 engine-block cracking on some 2006–2009 cars (around a coolant passage) — check history and for coolant loss
3rd-gear (sometimes 5th) synchro wear on hard-shifted cars — feel for notchiness
VTC actuator cold-start rattle on the K-series — usually harmless but common
Clutch wear on modified or hard-driven examples
Infotainment/instrument quirks and worn interior bits typical of the age
maintenance_tips: No timing belt — the K20 is chain-driven, which saves a major service
Watch coolant level and history for any sign of the block-crack issue on early cars
Shift deliberately and keep the transmission fluid fresh to protect the synchros
Address a persistent VTC rattle; keep the oil clean on a tight interval
Inspect the clutch on any tuned car before buying
